---
title: "Generative AI Seeped into Research Peer Reviews"
description: "10.6% of ICLR 2024 and 16.9% of EMNLP 2023 reviews are significantly modified by AI — and the chain of accountability is fraying."
author: "synesis"
date: "2024-03-27"
categories: [AI, LLMs, NLP, research, paper, generative AI, links]
image: cover.png
---

A while ago Wired wrote about how ChatGPT and the other similar GenerativeAI tools are now deployed to mass-produce scammy books flooding online book markets [[1]](#ref-1). The story seems straightforward: there’s a profit motive and there’s a tool enabling it, voila.

But that can never happen to ethics-conscious, integrity-driven researchers, can it? It turns out it absolutely can, albeit as of this writing, probably at a smaller scale (?) [[2]](#ref-2).

But if authors of research papers do it, the group that should hold them accountable, aka the reviewers, surely would not take the shorcuts? Well, according to a recent paper [[3]](#ref-3), 10.6% of ICLR2024 and 16.9% of EMNLP2023 reviews are significantly modified by AI.

(see article)

REFERENCES

*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_generativeai-iclr2024-emnlp2023-activity-7178800104579952640-DJZj).*

---

## References

<a id="ref-1"></a>[1] Kate Knibbs. "Scammy AI-Generated Book Rewrites Are Flooding Amazon." *Wired*, January 10, 2024. <https://www.wired.com/story/scammy-ai-generated-books-flooding-amazon/>

<a id="ref-2"></a>[2] Benjamin Han. "Research Papers Claiming 'I Am an AI Language Model.'" LinkedIn, 2024. <https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_elen-le-foll-elenlefoll-activity-7174624268310261760-eW0M>

<a id="ref-3"></a>[3] Weixin Liang, Zachary Izzo, Yaohui Zhang, Haley Lepp, Hancheng Cao, Xuandong Zhao, Lingjiao Chen, Haotian Ye, Sheng Liu, Zhi Huang, Daniel McFarland, and James Zou. "Monitoring AI-Modified Content at Scale: A Case Study on the Impact of ChatGPT on AI Conference Peer Reviews." 2024. <https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.07183>

<a id="ref-4"></a>[4] Jordan Boyd-Graber, Naoaki Okazaki, Anna Rogers. "ACL 2023 Policy on AI Writing Assistance." 2023. <https://2023.aclweb.org/blog/ACL-2023-policy/>

<a id="ref-5"></a>[5] Anna Rogers, Jordan Boyd-Graber, Naoaki Okazaki. "ACL'23 Peer Review Policies." 2023. <https://2023.aclweb.org/blog/ACL-2023-policy/>
